address: Khao San Road, Banglamphu, Phra Nakhon
best_time_to_visit: Evening between 18:00 and 23:00 when the street closes to traffic and the night market, bars, and street food stalls are in full swing

description.en:
## Overview

Khao San Road is Bangkok's most iconic backpacker strip — a 410-meter stretch in the historic Banglamphu district that has drawn budget travelers, gap-year adventurers, and curious locals since the 1980s. By day it is a relatively quiet lane of guesthouses and travel agencies; after dark it transforms into a sensory overload of neon signs, thumping music, sizzling woks, and market stalls selling everything from handmade jewelry to scorpion-on-a-stick.

## History

The road was originally a rice market — "khao san" literally means "milled rice" — serving the nearby royal district. After the publication of Alex Garland's novel *The Beach* in 1996 and the Leonardo DiCaprio film adaptation in 2000, the street cemented its reputation as Southeast Asia's ultimate backpacker hub. A major renovation in 2018 widened sidewalks, improved drainage, and added heritage-style lamp posts, giving the strip a cleaner look while keeping its chaotic charm.

## What to See

Street food is the main draw. Pad Thai vendors charge around 70-100 THB per plate, mango sticky rice goes for 60-100 THB, and adventurous eaters can try deep-fried insects for 40-60 THB. Beyond food, the road is lined with bars offering cheap buckets and live music, tattoo studios, massage parlors, and stalls selling elephant-print pants and vintage band tees. The surrounding Banglamphu neighborhood rewards exploration: Wat Chana Songkhram sits just across the street, and the Grand Palace and Wat Phra Kaew are a 15-minute walk south.

## Practical Info

There is no direct BTS or MRT station at Khao San Road. The easiest route is BTS to Saphan Taksin, then a Chao Phraya Express Boat to Phra Arthit Pier (a five-minute walk to the road). Taxis from Siam cost around 80-120 THB with the meter. Keep valuables close — pickpocketing peaks in the late-night crowds. Bring small-denomination cash as many vendors do not accept cards. Insect repellent is wise if you head toward the riverside after dark.
description.nl: Beroemde backpackersstraat met budgetaccommodaties en bruisend nachtleven

tips: Arrive after 18:00 when the road closes to traffic and the night market comes alive — daytime is unremarkable
Take the Chao Phraya Express Boat to Phra Arthit Pier instead of fighting traffic in a taxi
Keep small bills handy and leave valuables at your hotel — pickpocketing is common in late-night crowds
